en.text: The thing that hath been, it is that which shall be; and that which is done
is that which shall be done
things; neither shall there be any remembrance
of things that are to come
with those that shall come after
concerning all things that are done
therewith. to be: or, to afflict them
. that which is wanting: Heb. defect
, Lo, I am come to great estate
than all they that have been before
: and, behold, this also is vanity
. to give: Heb. to draw my flesh with wine all: Heb. the number of the days of their life
in them of all kind of fruits
above all that were in Jerusalem
me: servants born: Heb. sons of my house
, and that of all sorts. musical: Heb. musical instrument and instruments
more than all that were before
? even that which hath been already
. even: or, in those things which have been already done
. that: Heb. that there is an excellency in wisdom more than in folly, etc
; and why was I then more
. happeneth even: Heb. happeneth to me, even to me
unto me: for all is vanity
whether he shall be a wise
, and wherein I have shewed myself wise
, and that he should make his soul
, that it was from the hand
. should make: or, delight his senses
To every thing there is a season
; to refrain from: Heb. to be far from
in them, but for a man to rejoice
to it, nor any thing taken
That which hath been is now
; and that which is to be hath already
. that which is past: Heb. that which is driven away
them, and that they might see
. that God: or, that they might clear God, and see, etc
? of man: Heb. of the sons of man goeth upward: Heb. is ascending, etc
. every: Heb. all the rightness of work for this: Heb. this is the envy of a man from his neighbour
alone, and there is not a second
; neither saith he, For whom do I labour
. who: Heb. who knoweth not to be admonished
; whereas also he that is born
, even of all that have been before
them: they also that come after
in him. Surely this also is vanity
it; for he hath no pleasure
is it that thou shouldest not vow
. at the: Heb. at the will, or, purpose
Behold that which I have seen
him: for it is his portion
. It is good: Heb. there is a good which is comely, etc all the days: Heb. the number of the days
. For: or, Though he give not much, yet he remembereth, etc
, and also that he have no burial
. than: Heb. than the walking of the soul
That which hath been is named
? all: Heb. the number of the days of the life of his vanity
not thou, What is the cause that the former
than these? for thou dost not enquire
concerning this. wisely: Heb. out of wisdom
: and by it there is profit
. good: or, as good as an inheritance, yea, better too
? before: Heb. not in thy time?
of this; yea, also from this
men which are in the city
For there is not a just
thee: take: Heb. give not thine heart
that thou thyself likewise hast cursed
: I applied: Heb. I and mine heart compassed
by her. whoso: Heb. he that is good before God
: counting: or, weighing one thing after another, to find out the reason
among all those have I not found
not that which shall be: for who can tell
him when it shall be? when: or, how it shall be?
to it. discharge: or, casting off weapons
over another to his own hurt
But it shall not be well
: (for also there is that neither day
it; yea further; though a wise
it, yet shall he not be able
them. I considered: Heb. I gave, or, set to my heart
All things come alike to all: there is one
among all things that are done
unto all: yea, also the heart
that they go to the dead
, neither have they any more a reward
; neither have they any more a portion
in any thing that is done
. Live: Heb. See, or, Enjoy life
Yea also, when he that is a fool
to every one that he is a fool
. in great: Heb. in great heights
. a babbler: Heb. the master of the tongue
what shall be; and what shall be after
him? is full: Heb. multiplieth words
every one of them, because he knoweth
all things. maketh: Heb. maketh glad the life
. upon: Heb. upon the face of the waters
of her that is with child
, either this or that, or whether they both
. shall prosper: Heb. shall be right
thing it is for the eyes
in them all; yet let him remember
thou, that for all these things God
, the grinders: or, the grinders fail, because they grind little
Also when they shall be afraid
as it was: and the spirit
. moreover: or, the more wise the preacher was, etc
: for this is the whole duty of man
. Let: or, The end of the matter, even all that hath been heard, is
